insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive enables developers to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers should update their Storefront API queries to utilize the new @inContext directive with the channelId argument. This will enable them to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, providing a more accurate and relevant experience for merchants and customers. When using the @inContext directive, developers should ensure they are handling errors and exceptions properly, as the API may return different data or error messages depending on the sales channel context. Additionally, developers should review their existing API queries and update them to take advantage of the new @inContext directive, if necessary.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and relevant data being displayed in their sales channels, such as online store or point of sale. This will enable them to make better-informed decisions and provide a better experience for their customers.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over the data fetched from the Storefront API, enabling developers to build more customized and relevant experiences for merchants and customers. By utilizing the @inContext directive with the channelId argument, developers can create more tailored and effective solutions for their clients.

also_worth_knowing.text: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics

updates.title: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events
updates.summary: Metafield triggers and additional topics are now available for Events, allowing apps to subscribe to events for various objects and metafield changes.

updates.title: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials
updates.summary: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, simplifying theme page building.

updates.title: Full-stack capabilities to power app analytics
updates.summary: Full-stack capabilities are now available to power app analytics, making it easier for app developers to build merchant-facing analytics.

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available, providing early access to new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: Hydrogen CLI version 13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports channelId, allowing for applying a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type now includes productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.

recap.headline: Shopify updates: new events, Liquid templates, and app analytics capabilities

themes.title: New Events and APIs
themes.detail: New metafield triggers and topics are available for Events, including Order, Collection, and InventoryItem. The physical inventory feature preview introduces new inventory APIs in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API. The Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId`.

themes.title: Theme and App Development
themes.detail: Liquid templates can now compose pages with blocks and partials, and the CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2. Hydrogen has been updated to version 13.0.2 with new flags for non-interactive shop and storefront creation. App Store requirements have been updated to require unique app names.

themes.title: Security and Compliance
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate. Apps that store cardholder data must be updated to use this certificate.

actions.text: Update apps to use new metafield triggers and topics
Update apps to use unique names
Update card deposit endpoint to use mTLS certificate
